def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    # Find the repeating shape + isolated outlier
    # Collect all non-bg connected components
    visited=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    comps=[]
    for sr in range(R):
        for sc in range(C):
            v=grid[sr][sc]
            if v!=bg and not visited[sr][sc]:
                stk=[(sr,sc)]; cells=[]
                while stk:
                    r,c=stk.pop()
                    if r<0 or r>=R or c<0 or c>=C or visited[r][c] or grid[r][c]!=v: continue
                    visited[r][c]=True; cells.append((r,c))
                    for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]: stk.append((r+dr,c+dc))
                r0=min(r for r,c in cells); c0=min(c for r,c in cells)
                shape=frozenset((r-r0,c-c0) for r,c in cells)
                comps.append({'cells':cells,'color':v,'r0':r0,'c0':c0,'shape':shape})
    if not comps: return result
    shape_count=Counter(c['shape'] for c in comps)
    majority_shape=shape_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
    majority_comps=[c for c in comps if c['shape']==majority_shape]
    outlier_comps=[c for c in comps if c['shape']!=majority_shape]
    # Find the expected positions based on period of majority comps
    if len(majority_comps)>=2:
        rows=[c['r0'] for c in majority_comps]; cols=[c['c0'] for c in majority_comps]
        from collections import Counter as C2
        row_diffs=sorted(set(abs(rows[i]-rows[j]) for i in range(len(rows)) for j in range(i+1,len(rows)) if abs(rows[i]-rows[j])>0))
        col_diffs=sorted(set(abs(cols[i]-cols[j]) for i in range(len(cols)) for j in range(i+1,len(cols)) if abs(cols[i]-cols[j])>0))
        pr=row_diffs[0] if row_diffs else R; pc=col_diffs[0] if col_diffs else C
        for r0,c0 in [(r,c) for r in range(R) for c in range(C)
                      if any(mc['r0']%pr==r%pr and mc['c0']%pc==c%pc for mc in majority_comps)]:
            if not any(grid[r0+dr][c0+dc]!=bg for dr,dc in majority_shape if 0<=r0+dr<R and 0<=c0+dc<C):
                for dr,dc in majority_shape:
                    nr,nc=r0+dr,c0+dc
                    if 0<=nr<R and 0<=nc<C: result[nr][nc]=9
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    visited=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    comps=[]
    for sr in range(R):
        for sc in range(C):
            v=grid[sr][sc]
            if v!=bg and not visited[sr][sc]:
                stk=[(sr,sc)]; cells=[]
                while stk:
                    r,c=stk.pop()
                    if r<0 or r>=R or c<0 or c>=C or visited[r][c] or grid[r][c]!=v: continue
                    visited[r][c]=True; cells.append((r,c))
                    for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]: stk.append((r+dr,c+dc))
                r0=min(r for r,c in cells); c0=min(c for r,c in cells)
                shape=frozenset((r-r0,c-c0) for r,c in cells)
                comps.append({'cells':cells,'color':v,'r0':r0,'c0':c0,'shape':shape})
    if not comps: return result
    # The majority color/shape = repeated pattern; minority = outlier
    shape_count=Counter(c['shape'] for c in comps)
    majority_shape=shape_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
    outlier_comps=[c for c in comps if c['shape']!=majority_shape]
    # Mark outlier cells with a new color that indicates the pattern
    new_color=1
    for comp in outlier_comps:
        for r,c in comp['cells']: result[r][c]=new_color
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    fg_colors=[v for v,n in Counter(grid[r][c] for r in range(R) for c in range(C)).most_common() if v!=bg]
    if not fg_colors: return result
    fg=fg_colors[0]
    # Find repeating period horizontally and vertically
    fg_cells=[(r,c) for r in range(R) for c in range(C) if grid[r][c]==fg]
    if len(fg_cells)<2: return result
    rows=sorted(set(r for r,c in fg_cells))
    cols=sorted(set(c for r,c in fg_cells))
    # Find row period and col period
    row_diffs=[rows[i+1]-rows[i] for i in range(len(rows)-1)]
    col_diffs=[cols[i+1]-cols[i] for i in range(len(cols)-1)]
    pr=Counter(row_diffs).most_common(1)[0][0] if row_diffs else 1
    pc=Counter(col_diffs).most_common(1)[0][0] if col_diffs else 1
    # Extend pattern
    pattern_set=set((r%pr,c%pc) for r,c in fg_cells)
    for r in range(R):
        for c in range(C):
            if (r%pr,c%pc) in pattern_set and grid[r][c]==bg:
                result[r][c]=9
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    # Find the single isolated shape (appears far from the repeating cluster)
    visited=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    comps=[]
    for sr in range(R):
        for sc in range(C):
            v=grid[sr][sc]
            if v!=bg and not visited[sr][sc]:
                stk=[(sr,sc)]; cells=[]
                while stk:
                    r,c=stk.pop()
                    if r<0 or r>=R or c<0 or c>=C or visited[r][c] or grid[r][c]!=v: continue
                    visited[r][c]=True; cells.append((r,c))
                    for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]: stk.append((r+dr,c+dc))
                comps.append(cells)
    if not comps: return result
    # Find the component in the "upper" half (isolated)
    upper_comps=[c for c in comps if max(r for r,cc in c)<R//2]
    lower_comps=[c for c in comps if max(r for r,cc in c)>=R//2]
    # Mark the lower comps that correspond to a repeated pattern
    if upper_comps and lower_comps:
        iso=upper_comps[0]
        # Find corresponding position in lower pattern
        ic_r=sum(r for r,c in iso)//len(iso)
        ic_c=sum(c for r,c in iso)//len(iso)
        # Find center of lower pattern
        lc_r=sum(r for cells in lower_comps for r,c in cells)//sum(len(c) for c in lower_comps)
        lc_c=sum(c for cells in lower_comps for r,c in cells)//sum(len(c) for c in lower_comps)
        # Mark intersection with 1
        mid_r=(ic_r+lc_r)//2; mid_c=(ic_c+lc_c)//2
        if 0<=mid_r<R and 0<=mid_c<C: result[mid_r][mid_c]=1
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    fg_vals=[v for v,n in Counter(grid[r][c] for r in range(R) for c in range(C)).most_common() if v!=bg]
    if not fg_vals: return result
    # Group fg cells by value and find the one with unusual position
    for fg in fg_vals:
        cells=[(r,c) for r in range(R) for c in range(C) if grid[r][c]==fg]
        if len(cells)<3: continue
        # Find rows with fg cells
        rows_with_fg=sorted(set(r for r,c in cells))
        if len(rows_with_fg)<2: continue
        period=Counter([rows_with_fg[i+1]-rows_with_fg[i] for i in range(len(rows_with_fg)-1)])
        pr=period.most_common(1)[0][0]
        # Project the pattern upward
        min_row=min(rows_with_fg)
        cols_at_row={r:sorted(c for rr,c in cells if rr==r) for r in rows_with_fg}
        base_cols=cols_at_row.get(rows_with_fg[0],[])
        r=min_row-pr
        while r>=0:
            for c in base_cols:
                if 0<=c<C and result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=9
            r-=pr
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    # Mark cells where pattern would extend but are currently empty
    for fg in set(v for row in grid for v in row if v!=bg):
        cells=[(r,c) for r in range(R) for c in range(C) if grid[r][c]==fg]
        if len(cells)<2: continue
        rows=sorted(set(r for r,c in cells))
        cols=sorted(set(c for r,c in cells))
        if len(rows)==1 and len(cols)>=2:
            pc=Counter([cols[i+1]-cols[i] for i in range(len(cols)-1)]).most_common(1)[0][0]
            c=cols[-1]+pc
            while 0<=c<C:
                if grid[rows[0]][c]==bg: result[rows[0]][c]=9
                c+=pc
            c=cols[0]-pc
            while 0<=c<C:
                if grid[rows[0]][c]==bg: result[rows[0]][c]=9
                c-=pc
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    visited=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    comps=[]
    for sr in range(R):
        for sc in range(C):
            v=grid[sr][sc]
            if v!=bg and not visited[sr][sc]:
                stk=[(sr,sc)]; cells=[]
                while stk:
                    r,c=stk.pop()
                    if r<0 or r>=R or c<0 or c>=C or visited[r][c] or grid[r][c]!=v: continue
                    visited[r][c]=True; cells.append((r,c))
                    for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]: stk.append((r+dr,c+dc))
                r0=min(r for r,c in cells); c0=min(c for r,c in cells)
                shape=frozenset((r-r0,c-c0) for r,c in cells)
                comps.append({'cells':cells,'color':v,'r0':r0,'c0':c0,'shape':shape})
    shape_count=Counter(c['shape'] for c in comps)
    majority_shape=shape_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
    maj_comps=[c for c in comps if c['shape']==majority_shape]
    if len(maj_comps)<2: return result
    # Find the expected position based on periodicity
    r0s=sorted(c['r0'] for c in maj_comps); c0s=sorted(c['c0'] for c in maj_comps)
    rdiffs=[r0s[i+1]-r0s[i] for i in range(len(r0s)-1)]
    cdiffs=[c0s[i+1]-c0s[i] for i in range(len(c0s)-1)]
    pr=Counter(rdiffs).most_common(1)[0][0] if rdiffs else R
    pc=Counter(cdiffs).most_common(1)[0][0] if cdiffs else C
    # Check for a missing expected position
    for rc in set((c['r0']%pr,c['c0']%pc) for c in maj_comps):
        for base_r in range(rc[0],R,pr):
            for base_c in range(rc[1],C,pc):
                if not any(c['r0']==base_r and c['c0']==base_c for c in maj_comps):
                    for dr,dc in majority_shape:
                        nr,nc=base_r+dr,base_c+dc
                        if 0<=nr<R and 0<=nc<C and result[nr][nc]==bg: result[nr][nc]=9
    return result

def transform(grid):
    return [list(r) for r in grid]
